forbiddenClaims: licensed, authorised, or registered in any market
specific guaranteed exchange rate or fee for a corridor
real-world corridor availability (the corridors are illustrative samples)
partner bank or network count not stated in the brief
transaction volume or savings figures not supplied in the brief

rubricAnchors.score1: Corridors are presented as live, guaranteed products with no illustrative framing and no primary call to action.
rubricAnchors.score3: Corridors are framed as samples but timing values read as guarantees, or the integration path competes with the primary CTA.
rubricAnchors.score5: Corridors are clearly illustrative samples, every timing value carries a not-a-guarantee treatment, and the integration path stays secondary to a single request-access CTA.

rubricAnchors.score1: corridor-selector, settlement-timeline, and integration-overview share no common direction or token system.
rubricAnchors.score3: Shared tokens across the three screens but inconsistent illustrative-only labelling or state treatment.
rubricAnchors.score5: A single direction system unifies all three screens, including a consistent illustrative-only badge and not-a-guarantee tag applied to every corridor and timing value.

rubricAnchors.score1: No actionable blueprint; engineers cannot infer corridor-selector, settlement-timeline, or integration-overview states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Partial blueprints with unresolved corridor-unavailable or corridor-loading-error states.
rubricAnchors.score5: Engineers can build all three screens directly, including the four corridor-selector states, three timeline states, and the integration-step-active and integration-unreachable states.

rubricAnchors.score1: A direct clone of an existing stablecoin corridor or FX comparison page with the same guarantees framing.
rubricAnchors.score3: Derivative of common corridor-table patterns but the illustrative-only treatment is visually distinguishable.
rubricAnchors.score5: A distinct treatment that makes the illustrative, non-guaranteed nature of the corridors legible without diluting the business-payments pitch.

rubricAnchors.score1: No accessibility handling and no corridor-unavailable or corridor-loading-error states.
rubricAnchors.score3: Baseline contrast and focus with partial failure states for the corridor selector.
rubricAnchors.score5: WCAG-aligned contrast and focus across all three screens, with explicit corridor-unavailable, corridor-loading-error, and integration-unreachable states that preserve the illustrative-only and not-a-guarantee labels.

rubricAnchors.score1: Licence, guaranteed-timing, rate, or volume claims appear unsupported on the page.
rubricAnchors.score3: Most claims trace to the brief but one timing value reads as a guarantee.
rubricAnchors.score5: Every claim traces to the brief, corridors are labelled illustrative, every timing value is not-a-guarantee, and no regulatory or commercial claim appears unsupported.

adjudicationNotes: The single primary CTA is request access; the integration overview must remain a secondary path, not a competing CTA.
Any licence, authorisation, registration, or guaranteed-settlement claim is an automatic fail on evidence-discipline, regardless of how polished the layout is.
Treat the absence of real corridors and timing as a constraint: corridors are illustrative samples and timing is an expectation, never a guarantee.
The corridor-unavailable and corridor-loading-error states are load-bearing for this case; an output that omits them fails implementation-clarity and accessibility-and-failure-states.
Do not prescribe exact copy, brand colours, or pixel-level layout; the brief fixes constraints, not visual identity.
